Title: 3 held over fatal shooting outside Johor entertainment centre
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Police have detained 3 suspects, including a woman, to assist investigations into a fatal shooting outside an entertainment centre in Taman Industri Jaya, Skudai, over the weekend.
A 27-year-old man was killed and two others were injured after gunshots were fired outside the club at 11pm last Saturday.
Johor deputy police chief Hoo Chu Han said the trio, aged between 26 and 39, were arrested in the state capital on Sunday as part of ongoing investigations.
"All are friends of the victim, a bachelor who works in Singapore. We are still determining the motive," he said.
Earlier, a 27-year-old woman alerted police after shots were fired outside the premises. One man died at the scene, while another sustained gunshot injuries.
Police believe a third victim may also have been injured but has yet to be identified after being taken away by acquaintances shortly after the incident.
The case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
If convicted under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder, the accused faces the death penalty or, in some cases, life imprisonment and caning.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Education ministry mulls stationing security personnel at schools following fatal crashes
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Education Ministry will consider a proposal to station security personnel at all schools to bolster safety following two fatal car crashes at schools over the past month.
Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the School Safety Management Guidelines were being finalised to improve existing protocols.
The guidelines are being looked at by the Education Institution Safety Reform Committee, which comprises experts from various backgrounds.
Asked about the need for People's Volunteer Corps (Rela) members to be deployed at schools as a safety measure, Fadhlina said the ministry would study the proposal first.
She said the ministry would ensure the guidelines remained relevant to current challenges in protecting students and staff.
She said the initiative was being driven by the committee involving representatives from police and the National Institute of Occupational Safety and Health (NIOSH).
The committee also includes representatives from non-governmental organisations and the community to ensure school safety issues are prioritised.
"We have also instructed teachers and principals to strictly implement safety measures; there is no compromise on safety issues," she said.
On April 12, a Form One student was killed when
hit by a car
in front of a surau at SMK Tanjong Mas in Kota Baru.
More recently, on May 5, a Year Two girl died when she was
hit by a van
at the main gate of SK Seri Maimon in Parit Sulong, Batu Pahat.
Earlier, Fadhlina attended the national-level launch of the 2026 MyLesen B2 Sekolah (MyB2S) programme, officiated by Transport Minister Anthony Loke.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit ends higher against regional peers, mixed versus majors
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed higher against regional peers and was firmer versus most major currencies today as investors monitored developments in the United States-Iran negotiations and upcoming US-China talks.
At 6pm, however, the ringgit eased against the US dollar to 3.9220/9260 from 3.9185/9230 at last Friday's close.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said market attention remained focused on the United States-Iran negotiations, which had shown little progress.
He said investors were also awaiting the high-stakes meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on May 14-15, 2026.
"Meanwhile, the US Federal Reserve (Fed) appears unlikely to cut interest rates in the near term, while the appointment of new Fed chairman Kevin Warsh will also be closely monitored, particularly his inclination to balance growth and inflation," he told Bernama.
At the close, the ringgit traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.
It appreciated against the Japanese yen to 2.4955/4983 from 2.5010/5040 and strengthened versus the British pound to 5.3331/3383 from 5.3354/3416, but eased against the euro to 4.6150/6197 from 4.6121/6174 at last Friday's close.
The local currency also traded higher against regional peers.
It rose against the Singapore dollar to 3.0887/0921 from 3.0910/0948, strengthened versus the Thai baht to 12.0900/1083 from 12.1640/1844, gained against the Indonesian rupiah to 225.2/225.5 from 225.4/225.7, and advanced against the Philippine peso to 6.41/6.42 from 6.46/6.47 previously.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Rest N Go' massage chair operator offers 126.1mil new shares ahead of listing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: RNG Tech Bhd has signed an underwriting agreement with M&A Securities Sdn Bhd as part of its initial public offering (IPO) en route to the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia.
RNG Tech is expected to be listed by the third quarter of 2026.
The company mainly engaged in providing vending massage services under the "Rest N Go" brand.
The IPO involves the issuance of 126.1 million new shares, a 16 per cent of the enlarged issued share capital, as well as an offer for sale of 78.8 million offer ahares, about 10 per cent of the enlarged issued share capital.
Of the new shares, 39.4 million will be made available to the public via balloting, 11.8 million will be allocated to eligible directors, employees and contributors to the company under the pink form allocation.
Meanwhile, 74.9 million shares will be placed to selected Bumiputera investors approved by the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry.
For the offer shares, 23.6 million will be allocated to selected Bumiputera investors approved by the ministry via private placement, while the remaining 55.2 million shares will be placed to selected investors.
Under the underwriting agreement, M&A Securities will underwrite a total of 51.2 million new shares allocated to the Malaysian public and Pink Form applicants.
RNG Tech managing director Datin Sophia Tan Sok Fei said the agreement marks a key milestone in the company's IPO journey.
She said the underwriting will support the company's plans to scale its network further, particularly in strategic high-traffic locations across Malaysia and regional markets.
"The IPO will enable us to accelerate our growth by expanding the deployment of vending massage chairs, enhancing customer experience at RNG stations and premium outlets and strengthening the visibility of the Rest N Go brand.
"With our established network, integrated digital systems and regional presence, we are well positioned to meet rising demand for convenient and accessible massage services," she added.
Based on revenue, the company is Malaysia's largest vending massage chair operator, commanding a 69.3 per cent market share in 2024.
The company delivers convenient, accessible and affordable on-the-go massage services through vending massage chairs located in high-traffic areas such as shopping malls, airports and highway rest stops.
Its operations are supported by an integrated digital ecosystem comprising a meter reading system and the RNG mobile application, enabling real-time remote monitoring, cashless payments and enhanced customer engagement.
Since installing its first RNG station at Selayang Mall, Selangor in 2009, the company has expanded across Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Cambodia and Brunei.
As at July 31 2025, it owned and operated 4,146 vending massage chairs across these five countries.
A further 2,340 chairs are deployed in Vietnam and the Philippines under licensing arrangements.
M&A Securities is the adviser, sponsor, underwriter and placement agent for the IPO.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Umno remains relevant as more youth sign up online
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno is receiving a positive signal as more young people choose to join the party through its online membership registration platform, said its president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
He said the trend proves that the party, which is now 80 years old, continues to move in step with the times and remains trusted as an important platform in defending the country's stability and future.
"As president, one of the things that makes me happiest is seeing more young people choosing to join Umno through online membership registration.
"This signals that Umno continues to move in line with changing times and is still trusted as an important platform in safeguarding the country's stability and future," he said in a Facebook post today in conjunction with Umno's 80th anniversary celebration.
Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said although the party has gone through various ups and downs over the past eight decades, Umno's true strength lies not merely in its name, but in the loyalty of its grassroots members.
"We have been tested by political challenges, divisions, public perception and changing times. Yet Umno continues to stand strong because its strength lies in the fighting spirit of its members, who remain loyal in defending religion, race and the country," he said.
He said the continuity of the party's struggle requires a strong spirit of togetherness to ensure that every weakness can be corrected for the sake of future generations.
He also expressed appreciation to the thousands of members who attended the Umno 80th anniversary celebration at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur recently, which he described as a sign of strengthening the party's determination in its struggle.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NCT Group unit sells 40ha NSIP land for possible site by a global data centre operator
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: NCT Alliance Bhd's unit NCT World Sdn Bhd has signed a term sheet for the proposed sale of a 40-hectare land within the NCT Smart Industrial Park (NSIP).
NCT Alliance said the land has been identified by a global data centre operator as a potential site for development, reflecting Malaysia's growing appeal as a regional hub for digital infrastructure.
The interest also indicates the strength of NSIP's Managed Industrial Park (MIP) model, where integrated infrastructure, centralised estate management and long-term master planning create a stable and well-managed environment for large-scale, high-value investments.
The MIP framework provides clarity, consistency and operational reliability across the park, supporting efficient deployment and long-term scalability.
This readiness, combined with its master-planned ecosystem, has positioned the park as an attractive destination for global investors.
The proposed project is expected to support up to 800 megawatt of data centre capacity and will be developed by the buyer, subject to relevant regulatory approvals and the execution of definitive agreements between the parties.
Upon completion, the proposed sale is expected to contribute positively to the group's revenue and earnings, while strengthening its position in the digital infrastructure segment.
The entry of a global data centre operator into NSIP enhances the park's profile and reinforces its appeal to high-quality investors, supporting continued investment momentum across the development.
This builds on NSIP's growing recognition as a future-ready industrial ecosystem.
This includes its achievement of a five-diamond rating under the Low Carbon Cities 2030 Challenge and recognition as Malaysia's first Managed Industrial Park with IR4.0-ready facilities and GreenRE-certified ESG features.
The potential development reflects broader industry trends, with Malaysia continuing to attract strong data centre investments driven by artificial intelligence expansion and shifting global supply chains.
This has increased demand for strategically located, infrastructure-ready industrial land.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 11 elephants, including calf, freed from abandoned pond in Kinabatangan
Article:
KINABATANGAN: Eleven elephants, including a calf, were rescued after becoming trapped in an abandoned water pond near the Deramakot Forest Reserve at the FGV Cepat Ringgit B plantation here.
According to a statement shared by the Sabah Wildlife Department on its official Facebook page, 5R Hidupan Liar, the incident is believed to have occurred last week after the steep and slippery embankments of the pond prevented the animals from climbing out.
The statement said all the Borneo pygmy elephants were rescued by a wildlife team deployed to the site to carry out the operation.
"The team worked to create a gentler and safer exit path to enable the herd of elephants to get out of the pond.
"Thanks to the efforts and cooperation of all parties, all the elephants were successfully rescued with no serious injuries reported," it said.
The department recommended that the abandoned pond be filled in or fitted with safety barriers to prevent similar incidents in future.
It also expressed appreciation to all parties, including plantation workers, who provided early information that enabled a swift response.
"This proves that cooperation between agencies, the plantation and the community is vital in ensuring the continued safety of wildlife, particularly iconic Sabah species such as the Borneo pygmy elephant," it said.
The Borneo pygmy elephant is the smallest subspecies of Asian elephant, found only on the island of Borneo, mainly in Sabah, Malaysia. It is genetically distinct and is known for its smaller size and larger ears compared with mainland elephants.
It is typically found in forests and plantations in eastern Sabah, where habitat loss has increased human-wildlife contact.
Wildlife estimates suggest fewer than 1,500 remain in the wild, making it an endangered subspecies and a conservation priority in Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lorry congestion at KSAB to be raised with Home Ministry, says Loke
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Issues involving lorry congestion at the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB) in Tanjung Kupang, Iskandar Puteri, will be raised with the Home Ministry, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said today.
He said operations at the Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) complex, including the use of scanners, fall under the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA).
"This was previously handled under standard operating procedures and has now been taken over by them. It is something we are constantly monitoring, and the Transport Ministry will provide feedback to the Home Ministry," he said.
"We are aware that congestion at the Johor Causeway and KSAB is a major concern. The government has a special cabinet committee to handle this, and all operational issues will be discussed and managed through that platform," he added.
Loke said this at a press conference after launching the 2026 MyLesen B2 School (MyB2S) programme at the Austin International Convention Centre here.
Earlier,
it was reported that
hundreds of lorries entering Singapore were stranded for up to four hours on the Malaysia-Singapore Second Link (Linkedua).
The delays, which reportedly began in mid-February, were allegedly caused by mandatory scanning procedures for all heavy vehicles at KSAB.
It is understood that the complex currently operates only one scanner for Singapore-bound lorries, while Singapore has six scanners for vehicles entering the republic.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 11, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia extended its losses for a third consecutive session, weighed down by profit-taking in banking and telecommunications heavyweights.
The FBM KLCI traded cautiously due to weaker regional sentiment, as oil prices and global bond yields rose after reports that the United States and Iran failed to reach a deal to end the Middle East conflict.
The ringgit weakened to three point nine two one zero against the US dollar.
In the crypto market, Bitcoin slipped to three hundred seventeen thousand four hundred fifty nine ringgit.
Ethereum and Solana also eased to nine thousand one hundred sixty three ringgit and three hundred seventy two ringgit, respectively.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 60,000 B40 students to get free motorcycle licences under MyB2S
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Some 60,000 Form Four and Form Five students from B40 families will receive free motorcycle licences under the 2026 MyLesen B2 Sekolah (MyB2S) programme.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said RM18.5 million has been allocated for the initiative this year.
He said the programme was introduced following concerns over the high number of students riding to school without valid licences.
"This situation is not only illegal but also endangers students who ride without proper knowledge and skills. the government is concerned about students, especially in rural areas, who ride motorcycles without a valid licence.
"This initiative focuses on providing legal access to licensing while increasing competency and compliance with road laws among the youth," he said after launching the programme.
Also present was Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek.
Loke said there were more than 6,000 fatal accidents reported annually, with 65 per cent involving motorcyclists and pillion riders.
He said most of these fatalities involve those aged between 16 and 25, including school students.
He said the MyLesen B2 programme, introduced in 2023 to assist the B40 group through free or subsidised licences, continues to expand.
For this year, 30,000 participants are expected to benefit from the general MyLesen B2 scheme, involving a total allocation of RM10.5 million.
Fadhlina welcomed the strategic cooperation between the two ministries, saying the group training concept would make it more efficient for students to obtain their licences.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 6 arrested as Johor police dismantle 2 drug trafficking syndicates
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor police have dismantled two drug trafficking syndicates in separate operations last week, arresting six suspects and seizing drugs and assets worth more than RM4.8 million.
Also seized were luxury vehicles, including a BMW, and RM213,770 in cash.
Johor deputy police chief Deputy Commissioner Hoo Chuan Huat said in the first case, five men aged between 27 and 50, including the syndicate mastermind, were arrested in three coordinated raids in Permas Jaya and Bandar Seri Alam on Thursday (May 7).
Police seized various drugs, including methamphetamine, ketamine, Eramin 5 pills and ecstasy, with an estimated value of RM3.65 million.
"The syndicate used gated and guarded apartments as storage and repackaging locations before distributing drugs to the local market," he told a press conference at the Johor police headquarters today.
He added that preliminary urine tests showed 4 suspects tested positive for methamphetamine, while 1 tested positive for ketamine.
All suspects were remanded for 7 days until May 14.
Police also seized several vehicles believed to have been used in the syndicate's operations, including a BMW, Volkswagen Passat, Toyota Altis and motorcycles.
The syndicate is believed to have been active since March.
In a second case, police arrested a 21-year-old man at a terrace house in Taman Perling on Friday, with drugs and assets valued at RM1.12 million seized.
The suspect is believed to have been operating the illicit activity alone and allegedly used the residence as a packing and distribution centre.
He was remanded until May 15.
Both cases are being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
If convicted under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, the accused faces the death penalty by hanging, or life imprisonment with a minimum of 15 strokes of the cane.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Oil rises after Trump rejects Iran's response to US peace proposal
Article:
LONDON: Oil prices rallied on Monday, a day after President Donald Trump said Iran's response to a US peace proposal was "unacceptable", raising supply fears as the Strait of Hormuz stayed largely closed, which kept the global market tight.
Brent crude futures climbed US$2.70 or 2.67 per cent to US$103.99 a barrel at 0902 GMT. US West Texas Intermediate was at US$97.66 a barrel, up US$2.24, or 2.35 per cent.
They rose to US$105.99 and US$100.37 a barrel, respectively, earlier in the session.
Last week, both contracts recorded six per cent weekly losses on hopes for an imminent end to the 10-week-old conflict that would allow oil transit through the Strait of Hormuz.
"Despite reassuring noises that back channels are still open and the parties are talking, our take is that the US and Iran are as far away from agreement as when this supposed ceasefire started," PVM Oil Associates analyst John Evans said.
"We do not see anything changing before Donald Trump visits China and asks for Beijing's aid in pressuring Iran."
Trump is scheduled to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday and is expected to discuss Iran among other topics with Chinese President Xi Jinping, according to US officials.
The world has lost about 1 billion barrels of oil over the past two months and energy markets will take time to stabilise even if flows resume, Saudi Aramco chief executive officer Amin Nasser said on Sunday.
"Our bullish view remains and we align with Saudi Aramco's opinion that even if Hormuz is settled and opened, it will take many months for normality in oil supply to break out," Evans said.
Saudi Arabian crude oil exports to China are expected to fall further in June after buyers cut nominations because of costly prices linked to the US-Iran conflict and lower supplies, trade sources told Reuters.
Meanwhile, three tankers carrying crude exited the Strait of Hormuz last week and on Sunday with trackers switched off to avoid Iranian attacks, Kpler shipping data showed.
One was loaded with Iraqi crude and bound for Vietnam. Japan's industry ministry said a tanker carrying Azerbaijani crude oil was set to arrive as early as Tuesday, the first cargo of oil received from Central Asia since the Iran war began.
ANZ analysts expected Brent to remain above US$90 per barrel through 2026 and around US$80 to US$85 per barrel into 2027 as demand growth resumes and inventories are gradually rebuilt.
In an attempt to hedge prices and ensure revenue, US producer Diamondback Energy bought options to sell the price difference between US West Texas Intermediate crude and Brent at around minus US$42 a barrel in the coming months, a bet that could pay off if the US banned oil exports.
This would lead to a rise in domestic inventory as US refiners typically process less domestic crude than is produced in the country and would push down WTI prices and widen its discount to Brent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Urban T20 earners question fairness of proposed RON95 subsidy rationalisation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Some urban T20 earners have questioned the fairness of the government's proposed RON95 fuel subsidy rationalisation, arguing that it would further increase living costs for households in major cities.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim recently said the proposal for
targeted RON95 subsidy rationalisation
was still being refined by the National Economic Action Council's Crisis Management Task Force, including discussions on whether it should apply to the T5, T10, T15 or T20 income groups.
Dahlia, who only wanted to be identified by her first name, said the government should first review and redefine the country's income classifications, particularly the broad range within the T15 and T20 groups, before implementing targeted subsidy measures.
"Before making the decision, I think the government needs to restructure and redefine the income groups. The margins for the T15 and T20 are too wide," said the 53-year-old mother of two.
She added that many households classified as T20 were not necessarily financially comfortable, especially in urban centres such as Kuala Lumpur where living expenses remained high.
On targeted subsidies, she said the move was unfair as T20 earners were among the country's largest contributors to income tax.
"The T20 are the largest contributors to income tax. The incentive of income tax relief is diminishing. Not all T20s are wealthy households," she said.
Dahlia said the removal of fuel subsidies would directly affect household spending and force families to reassess essential commitments.
"I would have to reconsider essential expenditures such as insurance coverage, the use of my own transport, and educational resources for my children, including private co-curricular activities," she said.
She added that T20 groups should not be excluded entirely from government assistance.
"They are Malaysians too and deserve some, if not all, of the benefits of RON95 fuel subsidies," she said.
Meanwhile, Suhana Mohd Yusop, 44, said ordinary Malaysians often felt their concerns were ignored.
"It does not feel fair, but what can we do?" she said.
She added that some decisions appeared to benefit certain parties rather than the wider public.
Suhana also said the removal of fuel subsidies would inevitably increase the financial burden on consumers, as higher fuel costs would affect the prices of goods and services.
"Wherever you live, even if the increase is small, it will still affect you," she said.
Experts have also stressed that any subsidy rationalisation must be
supported by accurate and granular data,
warning that weak classification systems could result in households under genuine financial pressure being wrongly grouped into higher income bands.
Similar sentiments were echoed on social media, where many users argued that the T20 classification did not necessarily reflect financial comfort, particularly for urban households with children.
Threads user @issa.rence said many people had romanticised the "T20" label without understanding the financial realities faced by families living in Kuala Lumpur.
The user said a father of three living in the capital could be left with only about RM200 a month after paying for basic necessities, leaving little room for savings or leisure spending.
"With the petrol subsidy removed, instantly negative cash flow every month," the user wrote.
Another user, @simultaneous113, said the T20 income threshold did not necessarily mean households were wealthy.
"T20 is just a household income of RM11,820 a month or above. If a couple earning RM5,910 a month is considered rich, it is barely enough to survive if they have one or two children," the user said.
Facebook user Mohamad Ferdaouse Ismail also pointed out that T20 classification was based on combined household income rather than individual salaries.
"T20 is household income, not individual. So if a husband and wife each earn RM6,000 a month, they are already considered T20. Many middle-grade civil servants would also fall into this category. The socio-economic impact caused by the elimination of petrol subsidies on this group is huge," he said.
Others called on the government to reduce spending involving political leaders before removing public subsidies.
"All members of parliament should be the ones to have their subsidies revoked because their allowances and salaries exceed RM20,000," wrote social media user @Shah Black.
Another commenter, @Chong Kah Choon, said: "Cut ministers' salaries and allowances first."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang opposition questions CM Inc's unaudited accounts since 2009
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The state opposition has questioned Chief Minister Incorporated's (CMI) financial transparency, alleging the state-linked entity has never tabled complete audited accounts since 2009 despite managing millions in revenue.
Penang opposition leader Muhammad Fauzi Yusoff (Pas-Sungai Dua) claimed that CMI managed 71 projects in 2024 and 2025, while generating RM8.43 million in revenue from land rentals and lease agreements under the management of CMI and State Secretary Incorporated (SSI) as of Sept 30, 2025.
Fauzi, who is also chairman of the Penang Public Accounts Committee (PAC), said no comprehensive audited accounts had been made available to enable transparent scrutiny of the entity's financial management.
CMI operates under a public-private-professional partnership model (4P) to maximise the strategic value of state assets, particularly land, and buildings.
"Under amendments to the CMI Enactment, the corporation is required to maintain proper accounts and financial records, and prepare statements of accounts for every financial year.
"The chief minister must also ensure that copies of the statements, together with the auditor general's observations, are tabled at the state legislative assembly," he said at the ongoing state legislative assembly sitting here.
However, the state Pas commissioner alleged that the requirement had yet to be fully complied with, as the relevant financial statements and auditor general's observations had not been tabled before the assembly.
"How can an entity managing state revenue worth millions of ringgit continue operating without proper financial transparency?
"This is not merely a question of perception, but a matter involving the fundamental principles of good governance," he added.
Fauzi also questioned what he described as CMI's opaque management structure despite its extensive involvement in state-owned land and assets.
He said CMI was not only involved in small-scale projects, but also strategic land leases, joint ventures with developers and multiple development initiatives undertaken through special purpose vehicles (SPVs).
He said the public still lacked a clear understanding of the network of companies linked to CMI, including their shareholding structures and the actual ownership relationships of the entities involved.
"This situation raises questions over whether state assets are being managed transparently and who are the real beneficiaries of these projects.
"It also runs contrary to the CAT principles of competency, accountability and transparency, which the state government frequently champions.
"If the government truly upholds CAT, where is the competency when accounts remain unclear, where is the accountability when audits are not carried out, and where is the transparency when financial information is not tabled before the House," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Orkim raises capacity with latest vessel, to acquire another this year
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Marine transportation company Orkim Bhd has received delivery of its latest vessel, Orkim Garnet, bringing its fleet size to 19, and increasing its total load capacity to 253,036 deadweight tonnes (dwt).
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the group said Orkim Garnet, formerly known as Rich Sunda, is a 2026-built International Maritime Organisation (IMO) Type II tanker with a deadweight capacity of 13,850 tonnes (mt).
"Designed to transport both clean petroleum products (CPP) and chemical products, Orkim Garnet is scheduled to commence its maiden charter voyage in the near term," the filing said.
Orkim chief executive officer Captain Cheah Sin Bi said Orkim Garnet enables the group to expand into chemical and palm oil transportation, allowing them to capture broader regional trade opportunities.
"The acquisition supports our ongoing fleet rejuvenation efforts by lowering the group's average fleet age, which enhances overall fleet efficiency, reliability, and competitiveness.
"We expect to acquire another IMO Type II tanker in the second half of the year, alongside the delivery of two newly built CPP tankers in 2027," he said.
He added that the tanker market fundamentals remain supportive, with elevated tanker freight rates continuing to underpin profitability, while disciplined chartering structures remain key to managing volatility.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Public split over RON95 fuel subsidy cuts for T20 group
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians remain divided over the government's move to rationalise RON95 fuel subsidies, with some arguing that assistance should prioritise lower-income groups.
Several members of the public have backed the removal of subsidies for T20 earners, saying the move would help ensure government aid reaches those who truly need it.
Azlan Senan, who frequents Bangsar Shopping Centre, said the policy was fair as B40 groups continued to require assistance amid rising living costs.
"I think it is fair, since the B40 group who really needs it can benefit from the subsidies," he told the New Straits Times.
Similarly, Zubaidah Zakaria said she supported the removal of subsidies for T20 groups in the interest of fairness.
"I think it is fair to give subsidies to those who truly need them, especially the B40 group. Most T20 individuals are not significantly affected by fuel prices, so it is reasonable for the assistance to be more targeted and directed towards lower-income groups who rely on it more," she said.
On X, user @___nulis questioned the extent of subsidy use among the T20 group, arguing that the policy change may have limited impact on them.
"Actually, what percentage of T20 people even take fuel subsidies? Most I know own supercars that use RON97 anyway, they don't bother about subsidies and won't waste time scanning their identity card at the station. Ineffective solution by the government," the user said.
Meanwhile, former member of parliament Sivarraajh Chandran, in a Facebook post, supported the rationalisation move, describing it as a progressive step to improve government spending efficiency.
"I agree with the rationalisation of RON95 subsidies for the T20 group.
"It is a progressive step to ensure national subsidy expenditure is managed and channelled towards infrastructure development and other people-focused initiatives," he said.
He also urged that the policy be extended to include cabinet members, state executive councillors, MPs, senators and senior civil servants classified as T20, calling for leaders to "lead by example."
"Let it be fair to all and prove that politicians lead by example before asking the people to follow policies that sometimes make life difficult," he added.
Another Facebook user, @ダニエル ブロロロ, supported the move, saying higher-income earners should be responsible for their own fuel costs.
"Yes… they are rich enough to pay for fuel," the user said.
Meanwhile, Threads user @cole.nexus said the policy would not affect their lifestyle and supported greater fiscal responsibility.
"Frankly, it doesn't have any impact on my lifestyle anyway. With great power comes greater responsibility. I don't mind contributing more," the user said.
Another Threads user, @goesbyname.x strongly supported full subsidy rationalisation, arguing that assistance should be limited to B40 groups only.
"I agree. Fully rationalised subsidies for B40 only… We already have The Central Database Hub (PADU) and MyKasih systems that can be used.
"Paying tax is mandatory for everyone," they said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu sacks two more members, suspends three others
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has sacked two more members and suspended three others, days after the party took similar action against several elected representatives and leaders.
Those sacked were Kelantan deputy speaker Datuk Mohamed Farid Mohamed Zawawi, who is also the Kok Lanas assemblyman, and Armada executive council member Wan Meor Safwat Naqiuddin Shamsudin.
Meanwhile, Mersing member of parliament Muhammad Islahuddin Abas, Bukit Bunga assemblyman Mohd Almidi Jaafar and Armada executive council member Mohamad Arib Abu Kasim were suspended.
The decision, which took effect today, was conveyed in letters signed by Bersatu disciplinary board chairman Datuk Mohd Radzi Manan.
The letters stated that the disciplinary board had met and decided to take action against them under Clause 22.5 of the party constitution.
They were found to have breached Clause 9.1.4 of the party constitution, as well as the party's code of ethics and conduct.
For those suspended, the party said they would be stripped of all positions held in Bersatu and barred from issuing statements on behalf of the party until the suspension period ends.
The letters also stated that those affected may appeal to the party's appeals board within 14 days from the date of the notice.
The latest action follows
Bersatu's recent suspension of five MPs:
Tanah Merah MP Datuk Seri Ikmal Hisham Abdul Aziz, Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor (Ketereh), Datuk Abdul Khalib Abdullah (Rompin), Nordin Ahmad Ismail (Lumut) and Datuk Rosol Wahid (Hulu Terengganu).
Also suspended were Chenderoh assemblyman Syed Lukman Hakim Syed Mohd Zain and Ahmad Man (Kuala Sepetang).
The party had earlier sacked two Kedah assemblymen — Dzowahir Ab Ghani (Suka Menanti) and Abdul Razak Khamis (Sungai Tiang).
Armada deputy chief Faiz Rahmad was also sacked.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK's Starmer vows to prove doubters wrong and stay in power
Article:
LONDON: Prime Minister Keir Starmer sought to quell a mounting rebellion in his party on Monday by vowing to prove the doubters wrong and stay in power to avoid plunging Britain into a new political crisis.
A growing number of Labour lawmakers have turned on Starmer after his party suffered the worst local election results for a governing party in more than three decades last week, prompting a former junior minister to threaten to seek a leadership contest if he fails to offer radical change.
Starmer sought to show he had got the message, saying in an impassioned speech that "incremental change won't cut it" in a country that has suffered two decades of economic stagnation and mounting social tensions.
"I know that people are frustrated by the state of Britain. Frustrated by politics, and some people are frustrated with me," he said in a speech in London.
"I know I have my doubters and I know I need to prove them wrong. And I will."
Starmer won one of the largest parliamentary majorities in modern British history in 2024 with promises to expand the economy, lower illegal immigration, and cut waiting lists in the state-run health service.
However, progress has been hampered by policy U-turns, perceptions among some in his party that he is unwilling to take difficult decisions, and a series of political scandals, contributing to some of the lowest approval ratings of any British prime minister.
The uncertainty has pushed up borrowing costs as investors fear that Starmer could be replaced by a more left-wing leader willing to borrow more - a rerun of the chaos that ensued in the latter years of the previous Conservative Party which struggled to govern.
The populist Reform UK, led by Brexit campaigner Nigel Farage, has now led national opinion polls for more than a year and at the local elections last week, the left-wing Green Party also made large advances in British cities.
Starmer said Labour could not afford to turn on each other when the country was facing "very dangerous opponents" who want "more grievance politics, more division, more pointing at Britain's problems, looking not for solutions but for someone to blame."
"If we don't get this right, our country will go down a very dark path," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump dismisses Iran's reply to peace plan, oil jumps as Hormuz closure persists
Article:
DUBAI/WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump's swift rejection of Iran's response to a US peace proposal sent oil prices higher on Monday amid concerns the 10-week-old conflict will drag on, keeping shipping through the Strait of Hormuz paralysed.
Days after the US floated an offer in the hope of reopening negotiations, Iran on Sunday released a response focused on ending the war on all fronts, especially Lebanon, where US ally Israel is fighting Iran-backed Hizbollah fighters.
Teheran also included a demand for compensation for war damage and emphasised Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian state TV said.
It also called on the US to end its naval blockade, guarantee no further attacks, lift sanctions and end a US ban on Iranian oil sales, the semi-official Tasnim news agency said.
Within hours, Trump dismissed Iran's proposal with a post on social media.
"I don't like it — TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE," Trump wrote on Truth Social, without giving further detail.
The US had proposed an end to fighting before starting talks on more contentious issues, including Iran's nuclear programme.
Following Trump's rejection of its demands, Teheran said on Monday it believed its proposal to end the war was "generous and responsible".
"Our demand is legitimate: demanding an end to the war, lifting the (US) blockade and piracy, and releasing Iranian assets that have been unjustly frozen in banks due to US pressure," Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said.
"Safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz and establishing security in the region and Lebanon were other demands of Iran, which are considered a generous and responsible offer for regional security."
Oil prices rose nearly 3 per cent on Monday on news of the continued deadlock that leaves the Strait of Hormuz largely closed. Before the war began on February 28, the narrow waterway carried one-fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas flows, and has emerged as one of the central pressure points in the war.
Trickle of shipping through Hormuz
While traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is at a trickle compared to before the war, shipping data on Kpler and LSEG showed three tankers laden with crude exited the waterway last week, with trackers switched off to avoid Iranian attack.
Sporadic flare-ups in fighting around the strait in recent days have tested a ceasefire that has paused all-out warfare since it took effect in early April.
[[nid:1437307]]
Surveys show the war is unpopular with US voters facing sharply higher gasoline prices less than six months before nationwide elections that will determine whether Trump's Republican Party retains control of Congress.
The US has also found little international support, with Nato allies refusing calls to send ships to open the Strait of Hormuz without a full peace deal and an internationally mandated mission.
It is not clear what fresh diplomatic or military steps may be ahead.
Trump set to discuss Iran in Beijing
Trump is expected to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday. With mounting pressure to draw a line under the war and the global energy crisis it has ignited, Iran is among the topics Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are set to discuss.
Trump has been leaning on China to use its influence to push Teheran to make a deal with Washington.
Baghaei of Iran's foreign ministry suggested China could instead use the visit to push back against US goals in the Gulf.
Asked about Trump's travel to China, Baghaei said: "Our Chinese friends know very well how to use these opportunities to warn about the consequences of the US' illegal and bullying actions on regional peace and security as well as on economic stability and international security."
Addressing whether combat operations against Iran were over, Trump said in remarks aired on Sunday: "They are defeated, but that doesn't mean they're done."
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the war was not over because there was "more work to be done" to remove enriched uranium from Iran, dismantle enrichment sites and address Iran's proxies and ballistic missile capabilities.
The best way to remove the enriched uranium would be through diplomacy, Netanyahu said in an interview aired Sunday on CBS News' "60 Minutes". But he did not rule out removing it by force.
Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian said in a social media post that Iran would "never bow down to the enemy" and would "defend national interests with strength".
Despite diplomatic efforts to break the deadlock, the threat to shipping lanes and the economies of the region remained high.
On Sunday, the United Arab Emirates said it intercepted two drones coming from Iran, while Qatar condemned a drone attack that hit a cargo ship coming from Abu Dhabi in its waters. Kuwait said its air defences had dealt with hostile drones that entered its airspace.
Clashes have also continued in southern Lebanon between Israel and Iran-backed fighter group Hizbollah, despite a US-brokered ceasefire there announced on April 16.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Late selling pushes Bursa Malaysia into negative territory at close
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Late selling pressure dragged Bursa Malaysia into negative territory at the close, reversing earlier gains as profit-taking in heavyweight banking and transportation counters dampen overall market sentiment.
At 5pm, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 2.75 points to 1,745.31 from Friday's close of 1,748.06.
The benchmark index, which opened 5.94 points firmer at 1,754.0, moved between 1,744.99 and 1,754.0 during the trading session.
Market breadth was positive with gainers leading losers 562 to 558. A total of 636 counters were unchanged, 897 untraded, and 12 suspended.
Turnover increased to 4.20 billion units worth RM3.17 billion compared with 3.31 billion units worth RM3.00 billion on Friday.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said the market remains cautious due to renewed tensions in West Asia after United States President Donald Trump rejected Iran's latest peace proposal, which pushed oil prices higher once again.
He said elevated crude oil prices and lingering geopolitical uncertainties are likely to keep investor sentiment somewhat restrained in the near term.
Nevertheless, the recent pullback appears to be largely driven by profit-taking rather than aggressive selling, suggesting that underlying buying interest remains intact.
"Persistent foreign participation and improving regional sentiment may continue to provide support to the local market, particularly in selected blue-chip and defensive counters.
"Looking ahead, we expect the FBM KLCI to trade in a cautious but mildly positive manner, with selective accumulation likely to emerge on weakness," he told Bernama.
Meanwhile, IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said despite the broader weakness in blue-chip stocks, buying interest in selected infrastructure, construction and industrial-related counters helped limit further downside.
Across the region, he said Asian markets closed mixed as investors remained cautious ahead of Trump's visit to China later this week, with markets closely monitoring potential developments surrounding trade negotiations and broader geopolitical tensions.
"External uncertainties and elevated global yields continued to weigh on investor sentiment, although domestic institutional support and optimism surrounding infrastructure-linked sectors helped provide some stability to Bursa Malaysia despite the benchmark index's decline," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China says it won't allow Taiwan to attend WHO's annual assembly
Article:
BEIJING/TAIPEI: China said on Monday it would not allow Taiwan to take part in the annual assembly of the World Health Organisation (WHO), which starts next week, as Taiwan said it would send a delegation anyway for meetings outside the actual event.
Taiwan is excluded from most international organisations because of objections from Beijing, which considers the democratically governed island its territory.
Taiwan attended the WHO's World Health Assembly (WHA) as an observer from 2009 to 2016 under the administration of then-President Ma Ying-jeou, who signed landmark trade and tourism agreements with China.
But Beijing began blocking Taiwan's participation in 2017, after then-President Tsai Ing-wen won office, over her refusal to agree to China's position that both China and Taiwan are part of "one China". Current President Lai Ching-te has continued Tsai's policy.
Taiwan to hold own events, meet experts
Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiankun said the People's Republic of China was the sole legitimate government of the whole country.
"In order to uphold the 'One China' principle and defend the seriousness and authority of the relevant resolutions of the United Nations General Assembly and the WHA, China has decided not to agree to the participation of the Taiwan region in this year's WHA," he said in Beijing.
Speaking in Taipei, Taiwan Health Minister Shih Chung-liang said it was a "shame" the island had not been invited, especially given the pressing health challenges facing the world.
"We should not be absent, and should be even more proactive about taking part," he said.
Shih added that he would lead a delegation to Geneva for Taiwan's own events and meetings with health experts.
Speaking next to Shih, Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung said he was considering going with him to Geneva. Taiwan's government rejects Beijing's sovereignty claims and says Beijing has no right to speak for or claim to represent the island's 23 million people.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fuel subsidy should remain for all Malaysians, says Penang opposition chief
Article:
GEORGETOWN: Penang opposition chief Muhammad Fauzi Yusoff believes that the government should maintain fuel subsidies across the board regardless of income group.
This was as he fears any change would have a direct impact on the cost of living and the country's food supply chain.
He said the government should consider a uniform and comprehensive subsidy approach, describing the retargeting of fuel subsidies involving the T20 groups as technically complex and potentially susceptible to manipulation at the implementation level.
"Technically, the implementation is difficult. How do you verify income at a petrol station to determine whether a person is T20 or not, or whether they are eligible for the subsidy or not?
"Such things can be manipulated. So it is better for the subsidy to be standardised," he said when met at the Penang Legislative Assembly building here today.
Commenting on the proposal to withdraw subsidies from the T20 group, he also noted that these groups were taxpayers and deserved to enjoy the same benefits when it came to fuel subsidies.
"If it is given equally, it is better so that all Malaysians can enjoy the subsidy — so it is better to do it uniformly and equally.
"The government can still obtain revenue from these groups through various other channels. I think that is fairer," he said.
Fauzi also said the government needed to focus on diesel subsidies, as they did not only involve ordinary consumers but were closely tied to the transportation, agriculture, and fisheries sectors, which formed the backbone of the country's food security.
He said farmers used diesel for tractors and harvesting machinery, while fishermen depended on diesel for fishing operations that could cost thousands of ringgit per trip out to sea.
He said the government should focus more on how to increase subsidy provision for fishermen and farmers, who were said to currently not be enjoying the full benefit of the diesel subsidy, thereby adding to their operating costs.
"I have received many complaints from fishermen and farmers who are severely affected by this increase in diesel prices. We also see that the gap in diesel prices between Peninsular Malaysia and Sabah and Sarawak needs to be addressed.
"So the point is we want the increase in fuel prices, especially diesel, to be brought down. In other countries, no specific group is exempted — everyone is treated the same," he said.
Earlier, media reports said the government was fine-tuning the appropriate dividing line between those who should continue receiving petrol subsidies and those who could be removed from the subsidy system, as part of efforts to implement a more targeted subsidy mechanism.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said this
needed to be refined
without causing a major shock to groups that still required support, and that the steps involved determining the income threshold more carefully and ending the practice of blanket subsidy distribution.
On Sunday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had said the government was still studying the best mechanism regarding the proposed revision of petrol subsidies for high-income groups.
He had said the proposal had been discussed by several ministries and relevant committees over the past few weeks, but that the government had yet to finalise whether it would involve the T20, T15, or other categories.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia manufacturing momentum strong despite mining drag, growth outlook lifted
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Analysts remain optimistic about Malaysia's industrial production index (IPI), which expanded at a steady 3.1 per cent, although it fell short of the median consensus forecast of 3.5 per cent due to a sharper decline in mining output.
Kenanga Research has upgraded its 2026 manufacturing IPI growth projection following stronger-than-expected production performance in the first quarter of 2026 (Q1), a momentum expected to continue into the second quarter (Q2).
The firm revised its forecast upward to 4.3 per cent from the earlier estimate of 3.5 per cent.
"This is partly underpinned by accelerating stockpiling activity as manufacturers seek to mitigate raw material shortage risks stemming from Middle East-related supply disruptions.
"Stronger output in the first half of 2026 could partially offset a potential slowdown in the second half should second-round effects from prolonged geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility intensify.
"Encouragingly, the latest manufacturing purchasing managers' index (PMI) rose to 51.6 in April, the highest level in four years, pointing to a solid start for Q2," it said.
Echoing Kenanga Research's view, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said global manufacturing activity improved in April, supported by stronger output and new orders, although some demand was driven by firms frontloading purchases ahead of potential supply shortages and rising costs.
HLIB said while supply chain risks may continue to weigh on Malaysia's commodity-related production, the electrical and electronics sector remains supported by the ongoing global tech upcycle.
Both firms maintained their 2026 gross domestic product growth forecast at 4.5 per cent, although Kenanga Research raised its first-quarter growth estimate to 5.1 per cent from 4.7 per cent, citing stronger manufacturing momentum.
"We expect growth to strengthen in Q2 at 5.3 per cent, supported by inventory accumulation and firmer services activity under Visit Malaysia 2026, which should help offset mining weakness.
"Nonetheless, we expect growth momentum to slow in the second half of 2026 should geopolitical tensions persist, the 2025 base effect weigh, and inventory restocking ease," Kenanga Research said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GOF Central Brigade arrested 335 migrants, 37 smugglers this year
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: General Operations Force Central Brigade policemen arrested 335 undocumented migrants and 37 suspected smugglers in operations against people smuggling between Jan 1 and May 11.
Central Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Hakemal Hawari said the arrests were made in 38 raids linked to 55 cases investigated under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and the Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Anti-Smuggling of Migrants Act 2007.
"The migrants arrested comprised 237 adult men, 96 adult women and two boys.
"Also arrested were 37 people believed to have acted as land and sea transporters," he said in a statement.
He said the 37 comprised five Malaysian men, 29 Indonesian men, two Indonesian women and one Myanmar man.
Hakemal said the operations covered waters from Selangor to Johor and were carried out through coordinated action involving elements at Op Taring Nyah 1 posts in Selangor and Johor, as well as the GOF Central Brigade's Task Force Delta.
[[nid:1437311]]
He said police also seized 20 cars, three boats, 57 mobile phones and other items worth about RM1.6 million.
"The GOF Central Brigade will continue strengthening strategic enforcement approaches to combat migrant smuggling and tighten security control along vulnerable routes," he said.
Meanwhile, Hakemal said the brigade conducted 159 raids in overall operations between Jan 1 and May 11 across Selangor, Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya, Negri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor.
He said 551 people were arrested during the period, with various seizures valued at RM242 million.
"We urge the public to act as the eyes and ears of the police by channelling information on migrant smuggling activities.
"Public cooperation is crucial to prevent the country from being exploited by criminal elements and activities that violated the law," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'No home left' for Gazans stranded in West Bank since October 7
Article:
UNDER the bleachers of a West Bank stadium, a dozen men from Gaza live in a former changing room, blocked from returning home by the war that erupted more than two and a half years ago.
Among those stranded is Sameer Abu Salah, 54, who had been working odd jobs in Israel's commercial hub of Tel Aviv, where wages are far higher than in his home city of Khan Yunis in Gaza.
He had then gone to Nablus, in the north of the occupied West Bank, where he is now trapped.
"I entered (Israel) only four days before the war," he said from the little space he had set up under the stands of Nablus city stadium.
"I was respected and honoured. Then the war happened," he added, referring to the devastating Gaza war triggered by Hamas's Oct 7, 2023 attack on Israel.
Abu Salah now makes a living collecting and reselling recyclables, sending money to his family after losing two sons to Israeli airstrikes.
"Look at me now – I live in a tent. We used to live with dignity, while here we've been thrown aside like dogs," he said.
He has made the most of his situation. He fashioned a dresser out of cardboard boxes and decorated his walls with Palestinian flags and a portrait of historic Palestinian leader Yasser Arafat he found during his street sweeps.
Counting all those stranded is difficult, but the Palestinian Authority's labour ministry said in March it had provided cash assistance to 4,605 Gazans stuck in the West Bank.
Though leaving the city's boundaries is tolerated, the men under the bleachers still fear it, citing friends who were stopped at an Israeli army checkpoint and sent back to Gaza.
"It's boring, but what can we do? We're in a jail," said Sameh, who came 10 days before the war erupted to get medical treatment for his son that was unavailable in Gaza.
His son returned, but Sameh, stayed behind to provide for the family.
All the men AFP spoke to in the stadium had lost their homes in airstrikes. They showed videos of their homes before and photos of the piles of rubble after.
Nahed al-Hilou, a Gaza businessman now living in Ramallah, is equally afraid of leaving the central West Bank city he moved to from Tel Aviv after the war broke out.
[[nid:1437289]]
Hilou, 43, left Gaza two days before Oct 7 on a business permit to find goods to import into the blockaded territory, where he had a restaurant employing 30 people in Gaza City's upscale Rimal neighbourhood.
He found his way to Ramallah, where he opened a successful downtown falafel restaurant to make a living, and above all feed his family still in Gaza.
"I turned to what I know: my work, my profession, something I love," he said.
He now employs nine people, all Gazans, and cooks Gaza-style: spicy.
Like all those outside, he worries constantly for his immediate family, who luckily all survived the war.
"We spent 20 days not knowing anything about them," Hilou said. Asked about the possibility of returning, he waved it off.
"Of course Gaza is dearer than here, but there, there is no home left, nothing."
According to the UN, 81 per cent of Gaza's structures were destroyed during the war, and its economy along with them.
The UN says that unemployment in the territory soared to 80 per cent after the war, while prices for goods skyrocketed due in part to Israeli restrictions on truck entries.
Israel still controls about half of Gaza, and Israeli fire has killed at least 846 people since the start of a US-brokered ceasefire in October 2025.
Shahdeh Zaarb, 45, is luckier than his fellow Gazans, for he holds West Bank residency, having worked there for the past 20 years.
From the northern Gaza city of Beit Lahia, known for its strawberry fields before the war, Zaarb has opened a farm in the West Bank city of Qalqilya.
But despite his relative freedom, Zaarb has not seen his children since 2021, and shares the same problem as the others.
"My children are in one place, I'm in another, and I can't bring them here because of the crossings."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippine lawmakers start vote on impeachment of presidential hopeful Duterte
Article:
MANILA: Lawmakers in the Philippines started voting on Monday on whether to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte, in what could create a major hurdle in her bid to run for the presidency in 2028.
A House justice committee last month found probable cause for her impeachment in a petition from activists accusing her of misusing public funds, accumulating unexplained wealth and threatening the lives of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr, his wife and the former House speaker.
With her ally-turned-enemy Marcos limited by the constitution to a single term in office, Duterte is the clear favourite to succeed him in 2028. She has apologised for helping bring Marcos to power in 2022 after they ran on a joint ticket and won the votes for their respective positions in landslips.
Duterte denies wrongdoing, and her legal team has described the petition as defective and a "fishing expedition".
If more than one-third of the lower house backs her impeachment, the Senate must convene a trial with its members as jurors.
Duterte faces removal from office and a ban from politics if convicted. The impeachment effort is the latest in a series of setbacks for the influential Duterte family, with the vice president facing constant pressure from her bitter feud with Marcos and her father Rodrigo Duterte awaiting trial at the International Criminal Court over a war on drugs that killed thousands of people during his 2016-2022 presidency.
The House last year voted to impeach Sara Duterte, but that was later struck down by the Supreme Court over a procedural flaw.
[[nid:1437284]]
Duterte loyalist installed
In what could be a boost for Duterte, there was drama on Monday in the upper house Senate, where a motion was passed to remove its president and replace him with Alan Peter Cayetano, a staunch loyalist of her family.
The change in Senate leadership means Cayetano, a former running mate of Duterte's father, would be the presiding judge if an impeachment trial is convened.
Sara Duterte could become the highest-ranking official to be impeached in the Philippines after former President Joseph Estrada in 2000, whose trial was aborted after prosecutors walked out.
Three other top officials have been impeached, including an ombudsman and an election commission head, who both resigned before their trials.
Former Supreme Court chief justice Renato Corona is the only person so far to be convicted in an impeachment trial. Marcos survived a separate impeachment bid in February after his allies in Congress voted to dismiss it.
He has distanced himself from proceedings against his estranged vice president.
Dozens of supporters of the impeachment effort gathered outside Congress on Monday, chanting and holding banners saying "Impeach Sara Now".
Among them was left-wing Congressman Perci Cendana, who said he was confident there would be enough votes to back it, calling it a "historic day for democracy".
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran describes its proposal to end war with US as legitimate, generous
Article:
DUBAI: Iran's proposal to end war with the US and reopen the Strait of Hormuz was legitimate and generous, Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said on Monday, adding that the US continues to uphold unreasonable and one-sided demands.
"Our demand is legitimate: demanding an end to the war, lifting the (US) blockade and piracy, and releasing Iranian assets that have been unjustly frozen in banks due to US pressure," Baghaei said.
"Safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz and establishing security in the region and Lebanon were other demands of Iran, which are considered a generous and responsible offer for regional security."
"We did not demand any concessions. The only thing we demanded was Iran's legitimate rights," he said.
Iran's response to the latest US peace proposal focuses on "ending the war and maritime security", local media reported on Sunday, shortly after Teheran said it had delivered its answer to Pakistani mediators.
"It should be noted that the main focus of Iran's response to the US proposal is on 'ending the war and maritime security' in the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz," the ISNA news agency said.
The proposal emphasises the necessity of lifting sanctions by the US Office of Foreign Assets Control on Iranian oil sales during a 30-day period and ending the naval blockade on Iran, news agency Tasnim added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sarawak's push into aerospace sector can drive new economic growth
Article:
KUCHING: Sarawak's strategic move to venture into the aerospace and high-technology sectors has the potential to open a new chapter in the state's economic development, said Sarawak Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Dr Wan Junaidi Tuanku Jaafar.
The Governor said the industry could generate more employment opportunities, particularly for professionals and highly skilled workers, while offering competitive incomes.
"The aerospace industry includes the production of spare parts, aircraft maintenance, satellite technology, drone technology and the digital economy.
"With adequate education and training, I believe Sarawakians can compete internationally and position Sarawak as a respected high-tech and aerospace hub in the region," he said when opening the First Meeting of the Fifth Session of the 19th Sarawak State Assembly today.
Meanwhile, Wan Junaidi said infrastructure development projects, including the construction of bridges, coastal roads, the Sabah-Sarawak Link Road and roads connecting to Brunei, are expected to improve connectivity, shorten travel times and create wider economic opportunities for the people.
He also described the establishment of Sarawak's own airline, AirBorneo, as a significant step towards strengthening domestic and regional air connectivity.
"I hope AirBorneo will improve flight accessibility to Sarawak, including the state's interior areas. This effort can help stimulate tourism from outside and within Sarawak's rural regions while also stabilising flight costs to the state," he said.
Earlier, State Assembly Speaker Tan Sri Mohamad Asfia Awang Nassar said the current State Assembly sitting would include the tabling of the Sarawak Padi and Rice Board Bill 2026, aimed at strengthening the state's food security agenda through the systematic development of the padi and rice industry.
The bill is among four scheduled to be tabled during the eight-day assembly session, which began today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China starts building world's largest ice-snow text base for intelligent connected NEVs
Article:
BEIJING: China Automotive Technology and Research Centre Co, Ltd. (CATARC) has started construction of an ice-snow test base for intelligent connected new energy vehicles (ICNEVs), the world's largest of its kind, in north China's Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region.
The facility, which is scheduled to open in 2028, will also be the world's first such base capable of producing indoor snow for testing under simulated snowfall conditions,
Science and Technology Daily
reported on Sunday.
ICNEVs are vehicles that integrate new energy technologies with advanced connectivity, autonomous driving, and smart systems, transforming traditional cars into mobile intelligent terminals.
The new test base represents a leap in China's cold-weather NEV testing capabilities.
Spanning approximately 1,000 mu (about 67 hectares), the test base will feature core functional zones covering calibration of NEV three-electric systems, namely battery, motor, electronic control, chassis systems, simulated snowfall testing for intelligent connected vehicles, and flying car evaluations.
"The project directly addresses the industry bottleneck in extreme cold-weather validation," said Li Wei, vice president of CATARC.
"Once completed, it will break testing cycle constraints, improve industry standards, overcome low-temperature core technologies, and create a world-class validation platform. This will enhance China's comprehensive strength and international voice in extreme-environment automotive testing."
Wen Jinlei, vice mayor of Hulunbuir, the city hosting the test base, noted that the test base will promote the deep integration of regional ice-snow economy and automotive technology innovation, accelerating the exploration of an "ice-snow + technology" model.
The construction comes as China's NEV industry continues to expand rapidly. According to the latest official data from the China Association of Automobile Manufacturers, China's NEV production and sales reached 16.626 million and 16.49 million units in 2025, up 29 per cent and 28.2 per cent year-on-year, respectively.
However, cold-weather performance remains a critical challenge for NEVs. The new test base will enable precise control of snowfall volume, ice friction coefficients and other environmental factors, overcoming current limitations imposed by uncontrollable real-world conditions and providing a scientific, stable vehicle validation standard and data system, according to the report.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: T20 fuel subsidy review unfair to taxpayers, says Tuan Ibrahim
Article:
KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional has described the proposal for a more targeted subsidy programme, particularly the rationalisation of RON95 fuel subsidies for the T20 income group, as unfair.
Its deputy chairman, Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man, said the government must clearly define what constitutes the "rich" category before implementing any policy changes.
"The proposal will be unfair and will burden the T20 group, many of whom are taxpayers contributing significantly to national revenue.
"The T20 should not automatically be labelled as the rich. The government must clearly explain the definition of the wealthy group because the term is different from the T20 classification, which is generally based on household income," said the Kubang Kerian member of parliament.
Tuan Ibrahim, who is also Pas deputy president, said the proposal reflected the government's failure to fulfil its earlier promises on easing the people's cost of living burden.
"I see this as a sign of the government's failure in delivering on its promises.
"The rising cost of living, fragile and unstable incomes, as well as economic structural changes beyond the people's control, are among the main issues that must be carefully studied before any decision is made," he said.
Meanwhile, Pas vice-president Datuk Idris Ahmad said the definition of the T20 category itself was too broad and did not necessarily reflect an individual's actual financial situation.
"Gross household income does not necessarily reflect the real living conditions of a person.
"There are families categorised as T20 but living in major cities with high living costs, supporting elderly parents, raising many children, servicing education loans and handling other commitments.
"When subsidies are withdrawn across the board, it could punish groups that are not truly wealthy," said the Bagan Serai MP.
Idris also claimed the government appeared more inclined to cut subsidies than address leakages, wastage and inefficiencies in administration.
"The people will ask why subsidies are being targeted at the people first while issues involving project leakages, high management costs and wastage have yet to be comprehensively resolved.
"If subsidies are removed while wastage continues, the people will see the policy as merely shifting the burden of the government's management weaknesses onto the rakyat," he said.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had said the proposal to rationalise RON95 fuel subsidies for the T20 group was
still being refined
and expected to be finalised soon.
He had said the proposal was being reviewed by the National Economic Action Council's Crisis Management Task Force, including discussions on whether the subsidy rationalisation should involve the T5, T10, T15 or T20 groups.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said earlier today that the rationalisation of the RON95 subsidy will ensure that consumption, among other factors, is
taken into consideration
rather than relying solely on household income.
He said the rollout is expected to coincide with the beginning of a calendar month to ensure the technical screening system is fully prepared.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Transport Minister flags lorry lane congestion at Second Link, issue to be raised with Home Ministry
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 11 — The issue of congestion involving heavy vehicle lanes on the Malaysia-Singapore Second Link at the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB), which is alleged to be due to mandatory scanning inspections, will be brought to the attention of the Ministry of Home Affairs, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke.
Loke said that matters related to KSAB, including the operation of scanning machines, fall under the jurisdiction of the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS), which is under the Home Ministry’s purview.
“The scanner is under the Customs Department and was previously a standard operating procedure (SOP), and is now taken over by the AKPS. So this is something that we always pay attention to. The Ministry of Transport will provide feedback to the Home Ministry,” he said.
He said this at a press conference after the launching ceremony of the national-level MyLesen B2 School Programme (MyB2S) 2026 here today.
Loke said that the issue of congestion at the Johor Causeway and Second Link is indeed a concern for the government, and a special mechanism has been established through the Johor Causeway Congestion Cabinet Committee to address the issue comprehensively.
“All operational issues will be discussed, implemented, and managed through this platform,” he said.
Previously, the media reported that lorry drivers wanting to enter Singapore were stranded for up to four hours at the Second Link.
The congestion on the heavy vehicle lane has reportedly been occurring since mid-February, causing distress among lorry drivers who have had to face the situation until now.
The congestion was also reported to have occurred following the implementation of mandatory scanning inspections on all heavy vehicles.
The lorry drivers described the measure as a temporary solution, with some of them needing to walk about 500 metres after parking their vehicles to deliver documents at the KSAB before returning to their lorries to undergo the scanning process. — Bernama

Title: Prosecution moves to seize Range Rover and gold jewellery from ex‑Army Chief’s wife
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — The prosecution has filed an application to forfeit a Range Rover Sport SVR vehicle and jewellery seized from the wife of former Army Chief Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan, Salwani Anuar @ Kamaruddin.
The prosecution filed the application at the High Court here on May 4, naming 27-year-old Salwani Anuar as the respondent.
The prosecution is seeking to forfeit to the government a Range Rover Sport SVR, a 916-gold bracelet weighing 15.10 grammes, and three gold rings weighing 1.33 grammes, 1.41 grammes and 1.35 grammes respectively.
Also included in the forfeiture application is a pair of gold earrings weighing 6.640 grammes, valued at an estimated RM10,930, which were seized from the respondent.
According to the application notice, the assets were seized under Section 45(2) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLATFPUAA) and are now being held by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC).
Deputy Public Prosecutor Natrah Fareha Rahmat told reporters that during proceedings before High Court Deputy Registrar Zaridah Y Abdul Jaapar today, the court set May 19 for case mention.
Meanwhile, Deputy Public Prosecutor Mahadi Abdul Jumaat told Sessions Court Judge Rosli Ahmad that the prosecution had filed an application to forfeit 23 Rolex watches and 15 pieces of jewellery seized from Muhammad Hafizuddeain’s sister-in-law, Nor Shahira Atirah Anuar @ Kamaruddin.
Mahadi said the prosecution was also seeking a court order to publish a gazette notice requiring any party with an interest in the assets to come forward and appear in court, under Section 41(2) of the MACC Act 2009.
Lawyer Aizul Rohan Anuar said the defence had no objection to the prosecution’s application.
The court then set June 26 for case mention for the attendance of third parties.
On May 4, the prosecution filed the notice against Nor Shahira Atirah, seeking to forfeit to the government 23 luxury watches and 15 pieces of jewellery seized from the respondent.
The application was made because the prosecution, under Section 41(1) of the MACC Act, read together with Section 376 of the Criminal Procedure Code, was satisfied that the movable assets seized by the MACC should be forfeited, as the prosecution alleged that the assets were obtained from, or are linked to, an offence under Section 165 of the Penal Code. — Bernama

Title: Thunderstorms and strong winds to lash 10 states, MetMalaysia urges public to stay alert
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to affect 10 states until 9pm today.
In a statement, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the areas expected to be affected are Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling and Kulim in Kedah; Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta and Kampar in Perak; Gua Musang, Kelantan, as well as Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman in Terengganu.
Similar weather is also expected in Pahang, affecting Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Bentong, Kuantan and Pekan; Selangor (Hulu Selangor); Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Kuala Pilah, Rembau and Tampin); and Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru).
In Sarawak, the affected areas include Samarahan (Simunjan), Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa, Betong and Saratok), Sarikei, Sibu (Kanowit), Kapit (Song and Belaga), Miri (Beluru, Telang Usan, Miri and Marudi) and Limbang.
In Sabah, the affected areas include Pedalaman, Pantai Barat (Papar, Putatan, Penampang, Kota Kinabalu, Tuaran and Kota Belud) and Tawau, as well as the Federal Territory of Labuan. — Bernama

Title: Bersatu expels Kok Lanas and Parit leaders, suspends Mersing MP and Bukit Bunga rep in latest disciplinary action
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 11 — Bersatu’s Disciplinary Board today expelled and suspended four more elected representatives and party leaders with immediate effect.
Those expelled were Kok Lanas assemblyman and Ketereh division chief Datuk Mohamed Farid Mohamed Zawawi, as well as Parit division Armada chief Wan Meor Safwat Naqiuddin Shamsuddin.
